source_ticket.title: Specify client context and evidence lifecycle target states

critical_findings: Evidence upload and review are service, DB and audit-backed.
Evidence request creation is not first-class.
Target object selection is label-only in upload and document-scoped in review.
Relationship and entity detail surfaces still contain display-only data.
Vault behavior is only partially backend-backed and can fall back to static evidence rows.
Client-safe summary lacks a traceable readmodel to accepted evidence, review and audit.
Operational UI must not render process, proof, gate, contract or meta explainer boards.

non_negotiable_ui_rule.rule: No process, gate, proof, contract, source-trace or meta explainer tables/panels/boards in operational UI.
allowed_ui_evidence: disabled controls with concrete recovery reason
real object detail, history, evidence or next action content
forbidden_ui_evidence: implementation/source labels used as product guidance

target_states.target_behavior: Entity, relationship and wealth context must expose downstream readiness as service-backed object state. Display-only static relationship/entity detail must not count as closure.
ui_acceptance: The user can inspect the selected object and see whether downstream use is available, incomplete or blocked.
The UI uses product-native fields, selected-object detail and disabled action reasons, not process/proof panels.
Display-only fallback content is removed from completion claims or replaced by an honest blocked empty state.
service_acceptance: Family, entity and relationship context readiness is derived from service/readmodel data.
The readiness output contains object id, object type, visibility, sensitivity and missing prerequisites.
Backend scope and visibility filtering stay tenant/role aware.

target_states.target_behavior: Sensitivity must be an operative classification output that can block or permit downstream evidence/advice use without implying release readiness.
ui_acceptance: Sensitivity state is visible as product-native classification on the relevant object.
Restricted or hidden context is shown as unavailable or blocked without leaking hidden rows.
Copy must not imply advice readiness or client visibility.
service_acceptance: Classification is derived from persisted sensitivity and visibility projection.
Downstream state includes `ready`, `incomplete` or `blocked` plus missing reasons.
Hidden rows remain undisclosed for restricted roles.
out_of_scope: New final sensitivity taxonomy unless already present in Prisma enum.

target_states.target_behavior: The app must distinguish requested evidence from uploaded candidate evidence.
ui_acceptance: Evidence can be requested or the absence of request support is product-natively blocked.
Upload does not masquerade as request creation.
The user sees the requested target object and required document type before upload/review.
service_acceptance: The request state has an object id, object type, requested document type, status and audit/evidence trail.
Implementation should first reuse existing `EvidenceRecord`, `EvidenceItem`, `DocumentLink`, `AuditEvent` and `ObjectType` models.
If a new `EvidenceRequest` table is unavoidable, stop before migration and request explicit approval.

target_states.target_behavior: Target object references must use object identity where sufficiency depends on scope.
ui_acceptance: Target selection is a controlled object selection, not a free label, for sufficiency-relevant uploads/reviews.
If no eligible target object exists, upload/review actions are disabled with a concrete product reason.
The selected target object is preserved through upload, review and vault detail.
service_acceptance: Upload and review accept and validate `targetObjectType` and `targetObjectId` for allowed object types.
Evidence sufficiency compares related object type/id against the required target.
Ambiguous labels are kept only as optional human-readable context, not as sufficiency truth.

target_states.target_behavior: Vault rows and detail must be backend-backed or honestly blocked; static fallback rows cannot be counted as lifecycle closure.
ui_acceptance: Search, filters and selected detail are backed by APIs/readmodels or show a product-native unavailable state.
No static evidenceRows fallback is used as proof of completion.
Details show document/evidence/review/summary state only when backed by service data.
service_acceptance: `/api/documents` remains backend-backed for document list state.
Evidence detail must include EvidenceRecord, EvidenceItem, Review and Audit/decision summary where available.
Projection and redaction rules remain active.

target_states.target_behavior: Client-safe summaries must trace to accepted, scoped, current and client-safe evidence, without claiming release.
ui_acceptance: The summary is shown only as review output or blocked/unavailable, never as client release.
The UI can show the source document/review/evidence state product-natively.
Summary absence blocks downstream use with a clear reason.
service_acceptance: Sufficiency review persists or exposes a traceable decision/readmodel.
Review summary links to EvidenceRecord, DocumentReview and AuditEvent/decision where possible.
`clientVisible` and release/export flags remain false in this epic.

implementation_slices.title: Implement client context classification outputs
required_outputs: relationship/entity display-only surfaces downgraded or backed
blockers: No new schema migration in this slice.

implementation_slices.title: Implement evidence request state with existing models
required_outputs: audit-backed request/update state or explicit no-migration blocker
blockers: Stop before adding a new EvidenceRequest table.

implementation_slices.title: Implement target object identity for upload/review
required_outputs: sufficiency scope check uses selected object identity

implementation_slices.title: Implement vault readmodel closure
required_outputs: backend-backed evidence detail fields or blocked state
blockers: Stop if a new read API is required and cannot be implemented within existing routes.

qa_expectations: manual UI overclaim review for changed screens

task_c2_definition_of_done.new_read_models_allowed: Allowed only through existing models/routes in this execution. New schema migration requires explicit human approval.
